summary: Frontend e2e testing of Holiday Calendar + Holidays & Comp-Off Settings tab as HR admin. All 21 review-request scenarios passed: Calendar nav placement, calendar grid + legend + side panel, holiday CRUD, duplicate-date 409 error, seed defaults for 2027, comp-off scan and approval. Routing guard correctly redirects unauthenticated users to /login.
critical_code_review_comments: HolidayCalendar.js builds month grid using `new Date(year, month, d).toISOString()` which converts to UTC and can shift the date by one day in non-UTC timezones (e.g., IST on early-AM browsers can produce previous-day ISO strings). Recommend formatting locally: `${year}-${String(month+1).padStart(2,'0')}-${String(d).padStart(2,'0')}`. Currently works in UTC test env but is a latent bug for India users.
HolidaysAndCompOffTab.js description input has no data-testid (test had to fall back to placeholder selector). Add data-testid='holiday-description' for consistency.
Year selector dropdown only offers todayY-1..todayY+2 (4 years). If admin needs to plan further out or revisit history, this range is restrictive.
test_credentials: admin/Admin@123 used. Tried RMF0001/Welcome@123 for RBAC negative test — login did not progress past /login page (could be wrong password or rate-limit), so RBAC was instead verified by hitting /settings and /calendar in unauthenticated session — both correctly redirect to /login.
seed_data_creation: Seeded 12 default holidays for year 2027 via 'Seed India Defaults' button during testing. Approved one pending comp-off (RMF0008, earn_date 2026-05-01 Labour Day). No teardown done (data is intentional admin config).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Calendar + Holidays & Comp-Off flows validated end-to-end. Pending in DB after this run: the 'Approved comp-off' is now consumed; 2027 holidays seeded so re-running 'Seed India Defaults' for 2027 will skip all 12. Add holiday Add/Edit/Delete validated with id 69f881794c7a90ca1017ff16 (already deleted). Duplicate Republic Day (2026-01-26) returned proper 409 with message 'A holiday already exists on 2026-01-26: Republic Day'.
